title: Brazilian Cinematographer Adolpho Veloso Celebrates Oscar Nomination for Train Dreams - Deadline
text: Brazilian cinematographer Adolpho Veloso, nominated for an Academy Award for his work on the Netflix film Train Dreams, expressed enthusiasm about the recognition his country is receiving. He noted that This whole Oscar thing is so big in Brazil that people are celebrating everything, like it's a World Cup! Veloso highlighted Brazil's unprecedented success this awards season, with five nominations total—including his own cinematography nomination and four for The Secret Agent. He credited Brazil's increased cultural visibility to social media influence and broader recognition of Brazilian talent beyond individual films. Directed by Clint Bentley and starring Joel Edgerton, Train Dreams required extensive location scouting across the Pacific Northwest. The cinematographer explained the difficulty of finding ancient, untouched forests suitable for a story about early 20th-century loggers. The team employed creative solutions, using fake trees and strategically angled shots to depict logging work without harming protected vegetation. The achievement proved particularly meaningful on a personal level. Veloso, a lifelong football enthusiast who has attended three World Cups, received a Corinthians jersey bearing his name and the number seven—honoring his soccer hero Marcelinho Carioca.
